title: Chitrangudi Bird Sanctuary
tagline: Chitrangudi Bird Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Chitrangudi Bird Sanctuary locally known as "Chitrangudi Kanmoli" is a .4763 km2 (0.1839 sq mi) Protected area declared in 1989 and a part of Chitrangudi village, Mudukulathur Talu
overview.description: Chitrangudi Bird Sanctuary locally known as "Chitrangudi Kanmoli" is a .4763 km2 (0.1839 sq mi) Protected area declared in 1989 and a part of Chitrangudi village, Mudukulathur Taluk, Ramanathapuram District, Tamil Nadu, India. It is adjacent to Kanjirankulam Bird Sanctuary. It is notable as a nesting site for several migratory heron species that roost in the prominent growth of Babul trees there. International name: Chitragudi and Kanjirankulam Bird Sanctuary, IBA Code: IN261, Criteria: A1, A4i.

topPlaces.name: Enathi
topPlaces.description: Enathi is a village in Tamil Nadu, India, located in India The village has many temples, such as Boongulam, Ancheneya, Kali, and some Vinayagar and Ayyanar. Kabaddi is a popular game in the village, and a number of well-known players are from here.

topPlaces.name: Mudukulathur
topPlaces.description: Mudukulathur is a Town panchayat in the Ramanathapuram district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu

topPlaces.name: Meesal Kilavaneri
topPlaces.description: Meesal (Tamil: மீசல்), also known as Meesal Kilavaneri (மீசல் கிலவநேரி), is a village in the Mudukulathur taluk of Ramanathapuram district in the southern Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The settlement is known for its history of religious coexistence and its association with Tamil Muslim scholarship.

topPlaces.name: Sellur (Mudukulathur)
topPlaces.description: Sellur is a village in Ramanathapuram district and Mudukulathur taluka in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Parukkaikudi
topPlaces.description: Parukkaikudi is a village in the Ramanathapuram district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Parukkaikudi is a small Village/hamlet in Mudukulathur Taluk in Ramanathapuram District of Tamil Nadu state, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chitrangudi Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chitrangudi Bird Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chitrangudi Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Madurai Airport (71 km) and the nearest railway station is Madurai Junction (77 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chitrangudi Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chitrangudi Bird Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chitrangudi Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Chitrangudi Bird Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chitrangudi Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Enathi, Mudukulathur, Meesal Kilavaneri, Sellur (Mudukulathur), Parukkaikudi.
